segments.text: Now you can study and work across 27 European countries like Germany, France,
Italy, and the Netherlands without the usual visa chaos. Following the India EU free trade agreement, the European Union has announced its first legal gateway
office in India, a one-stop hub for Indians who want to study, research, or work anywhere in the EU. But this isn't just about visas. It's about Europe's
demographic reality. Europe's working age population is shrinking. By 2030, it will need millions of skilled workers,
healthcare, and research. That's why the gateway office starts with IT roles,
directly connecting Indian professionals to companies in Berlin, Paris, and Amsterdam. For students and researchers,
this opens access to Horizon Europe, the world's largest public research fund with a 100 billion euro budget. The deal
also promises uncapped student mobility unlike the US, Canada and Australia which are tightening student visas. Will Indian students now choose Europe over
the US and UK? Let me know in the comments.
